title: Exact Duplicates, Near Duplicates, Change Detection, and Version History · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds multi-signal content identity and change ledger from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of multi-signal content identity and change ledger while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Compute and label wire, decoded, DOM, main-content, record, and rendered digests for distinct questions.
Separate URL aliases, byte-identical responses, equivalent admitted records, and replayed observations without deleting provenance.
Use tokenization, boilerplate policy, thresholds, examples, and false-merge review for similarity decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stage Specific Diges…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 2331 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate stage digests, exact identity, near-duplicate review, change classes, versions, and tombstones; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compute and label wire, decoded, DOM, main-content, record, and rendered digests for distinct questions.

What must be established before implementation?
